# Mark It Down

> A free Chrome extension — Markdown editor and Web Clipper that turns your
> browser into a workspace for digesting AI output. Paste from ChatGPT or
> Claude, clip any web page, rewrite in your own words, sync via Git, and
> export to PDF, DOCX, EPUB, HTML, or LaTeX. Works as a Side Panel beside
> your browser or as a New Tab. Bilingual (English / Japanese). No account,
> no server, no telemetry — everything stays in Chrome's local storage.

## What it is
- A Chrome extension Markdown editor that runs in New Tab and Side Panel
- A Web Clipper that converts web articles to Markdown with a right-click
- Obsidian-style `[[wikilinks]]`, templates, Git sync, RSS reader
- PDF / DOCX / HTML / EPUB / LaTeX / .md export
- Free, no ads, no account, offline

## What it is not
- Not a long-term note vault (Obsidian / Notion are better for that)
- Not a knowledge base (built for digestion, not storage)
- Not an AI assistant (runs in front of the AI, as a pre-processing layer)
- Not a cloud sync service (local + optional Git only)

## Philosophy
- Fewer tools, more whitespace (no plugins, no widgets)
- Digestion over storage (copy-paste doesn't make it yours)
- A workshop, not a warehouse

## Audience
Developers, researchers, and knowledge workers who use multiple AI tools
(ChatGPT / Claude / Gemini) and need a neutral pre-processing layer before
committing notes to their long-term system (Obsidian, Notion, etc.).
